✝️ Welcome to Dr. Patricia Bailey Ministries

South Africa Youth Mission Trip — Application & Information Package

✨
Thank you for your interest! This application must be completed in full by the youth participant and co-signed by a parent or legal guardian. All information is kept confidential and used solely for trip preparation and safety.
Trip Overview
🌍 Destination: South Africa
✈️ Departure: TBD by Ministry
📅 Trip Dates: July 6–13, 2026
👦 Ages: 13–18 years old
👨‍👩‍👧 Chaperone Ratio: 1:4
🏥 Medical Staff: On-site each day
Application Deadlines
📋 Application Due: TBD
💉 Vaccinations: 6 wks before travel
🛂 Passport Copy: With application
💰 Deposit Due: Upon acceptance
🎒 Orientation: 2 wks before travel
📞 Questions: Contact office
What to Expect on This Trip
Participants will engage in community outreach, children's ministry, evangelism, worship services, and construction/service projects alongside South African partner ministries. Evenings will include devotional time, reflection, and team bonding. This is a service-first trip — participants should come with a servant's heart and open mind.
⚠️
Required Vaccinations for South Africa: Yellow Fever (if transiting endemic countries), Typhoid, Hepatitis A & B, Tetanus/Tdap, Malaria Prophylaxis. Consult your physician at least 6 weeks prior to departure. Proof of vaccination is required before boarding.
This package contains 9 sections: Applicant Info · Parent/Guardian · Medical Release · Passport & Travel · Education & Employment · Packing Checklist · Get To Know You · References · Waivers & Signatures. Complete all sections to submit.

Step 3 of 9
🏥 Medical Information & Release Form

This section must be completed accurately and honestly. Medical information is kept strictly confidential and shared only with trip medical staff and leadership on a need-to-know basis.

ℹ️
For international travel, we recommend participants visit a travel medicine clinic at least 6 weeks before departure. Required vaccinations for South Africa may include: Yellow Fever, Typhoid, Hep A & B, Malaria Prophylaxis, and Tetanus.

📋
MEDICAL RELEASE AUTHORIZATION
I/We, the parent(s)/legal guardian(s) of the above-named minor, hereby authorize the trip medical personnel and leaders of Dr. Patricia Bailey Ministries to secure and consent to emergency medical, surgical, dental, or other care for our child in the event we cannot be reached. This authorization includes consent to hospitalization, surgery, anesthesia, X-rays, blood transfusions, or any other medical procedure deemed necessary by a licensed physician. I/We understand and agree that Dr. Patricia Bailey Ministries is not responsible for any injury, illness, or medical costs incurred, and that trip travel insurance will be the primary coverage while personal health insurance will be secondary.
Step 4 of 9
🛂 Passport & Travel Documents

All travelers to South Africa must have a valid U.S. Passport with at least 6 months validity beyond the return date and at least 2 blank visa pages. Minor applicants must have their own individual passport.

⚠️
Passport Requirement: Your passport must be valid for at least 6 months after your planned return date. If your passport expires sooner, apply for renewal immediately — processing can take 6–8 weeks. Upload a clear photo copy or scan of your passport photo page with this application.
❌
Passport Expiry Warning: Your passport may not be valid for 6 months beyond your travel dates. Please renew immediately before applying.
✈️ Flight & Travel Information

Step 6 of 9
🎒 Packing Checklist for South Africa

Review and check off items as you pack. This checklist must be acknowledged before submission. South Africa's climate varies — most mission regions are subtropical with warm days and cool evenings. Dress modestly for ministry work at all times.

🧳
Bag Limits: 1 checked bag (50 lbs max) + 1 carry-on + 1 personal item per airline. Pack lightly — you will be active daily. Leave room in your checked bag for ministry supplies and souvenirs on the return trip.
📄 Required Travel Documents (Carry-On ONLY)
👕 Clothing (Modest & Practical)
👟 Footwear
🧴 Toiletries & Personal Care
💊 Medical & Health
🔌 Electronics & Accessories
🎒 Bags & Luggage
💵 Money & Security
🙏 Ministry & Service Items
🚫 Do NOT Pack
❌ Weapons of any kind  |  ❌ Illegal substances  |  ❌ Immodest or inappropriate clothing
❌ Expensive jewelry or valuables  |  ❌ Large amounts of cash  |  ❌ Politically offensive items
❌ Aerosol cans larger than 3.4oz (carry-on)  |  ❌ Alcohol or tobacco
Step 7 of 9
🙏 VII. Other Questions — Get To Know You

Please answer all questions as fully and honestly as you can. These help our team best prepare for ministry together. From the River Church Johannesburg 2026 Mission Encounter Application.

Step 8 of 9
👥 VIII. References

Please provide two references of people who have seen you in a team setting and who are well acquainted with your strengths, weaknesses, and service abilities. We may contact your references.

📋
References should not be family members. Choose people who can speak to your character, teamwork, and ministry commitment — a pastor, ministry leader, teacher, or colleague.

Step 9 of 9
📝 VII. Declarations, Consent & Liability

I have completed all sections of this application. I understand this is necessary to protect Dr. Patricia Bailey Ministries from possible legal actions. All waivers must be read in full before signing.

1. General Liability & Risk Waiver

I/We, the undersigned parent(s) or legal guardian(s) of the minor named in this application, acknowledge that participation in the Dr. Patricia Bailey Ministries South Africa Mission Trip involves inherent risks including but not limited to: physical exertion, outdoor activities, exposure to unfamiliar environments, travel by aircraft and ground vehicles, interaction with local communities, and exposure to health risks abroad.

In consideration of the minor's participation, I/We voluntarily assume full responsibility for any and all risks, injuries, or damages that may result. I/We hereby waive, release, and discharge Dr. Patricia Bailey Ministries, its officers, directors, volunteers, staff, and agents from any and all liability, claims, demands, actions, or causes of action arising from the minor's participation in this trip.

I/We agree to indemnify and hold harmless Dr. Patricia Bailey Ministries from any financial or legal responsibility for accident, injury, illness, loss of personal property, or death that may occur during the trip. I/We also affirm that the participant is in good health and medically fit to travel internationally.

2. Code of Conduct Agreement

As a participant of the Dr. Patricia Bailey Ministries South Africa Mission Trip, I agree to abide by the following conduct standards at all times during the trip:

I will: Treat all people with dignity, respect, and Christ-like love. Follow all instructions from trip leaders and chaperones immediately and without argument. Represent Dr. Patricia Bailey Ministries, my church, and the body of Christ with excellence. Participate fully in all scheduled activities, devotions, and ministry work. Maintain a curfew as assigned by leadership. Notify a leader immediately of any safety concern or emergency.

I will NOT: Use drugs, alcohol, or tobacco in any form. Engage in any romantic or inappropriate physical contact. Leave the group without explicit permission from a trip leader. Post anything online about the trip without leadership approval. Engage in behavior that embarrasses the ministry or endangers others.

I understand that violation of this code may result in early removal from the trip at the family's expense, with no refund of trip fees.

3. Photo & Media Release

I/We grant Dr. Patricia Bailey Ministries permission to photograph, video record, and publish images or footage of the minor named in this application for ministry purposes including but not limited to: social media, website content, fundraising materials, newsletters, and promotional materials. No compensation is requested or expected.

4. Financial Responsibility Agreement

I/We understand and agree that: The trip fee must be paid in full by the deadline communicated by the ministry. Non-refundable deposits are required upon acceptance. If the participant withdraws after the full balance is paid, the ministry's refund policy applies and may result in partial or no refund. In the event of early removal due to conduct violations, no refund will be issued. Any additional costs incurred (medical emergencies, travel changes, lost documents, etc.) are the financial responsibility of the participant's family.

Liability Release
📄
A separate liability form is required as part of your application process.
Please download the liability form, sign it, and email it to missions@patriciabaileyministries.org at the time of submitting this application.
Declaration

I, the undersigned youth applicant, have read and agree to all waivers, codes of conduct, and policies above. I commit to this trip with a servant's heart and understand all expectations.
